setting.note: A single square, mapped to the door. James fixes the whole novel to a few blocks of Greenwich Village: Dr Sloper's wide-fronted house on Washington Square North, the real Row of Greek Revival houses that still stands; the Square itself, with its wooden paling and ailantus-trees; and the confident new Fifth Avenue taking its origin at the corner. Downtown, near the City Hall, is the old world the doctor left; uptown is the raw frontier fashion was chasing. A tour of Switzerland is the one excursion beyond the setting.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Henry James, first published 1880; the author died in 1916.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the United States (published 1880), and in the United Kingdom and the European Union, where the author died more than 70 years ago (1916).
rights.attribution: Henry James, Washington Square (1880). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The cold grey of Dr Sloper's eye, the ironic intelligence that presides over the whole book. A colour drawn straight from the text. Clears WCAG AA on paper.

catherine.name: Catherine Sloper
catherine.colorSourceQuote: a red satin gown trimmed with gold fringe
